response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled full-stack and systems engineer with broad polyglot experience, but he is not a match for this role. Mandatory C++ experience is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a thesis project; there is no sustained, production-level C++ work on Windows. SQL Server and Oracle are absent from his stack (MongoDB and MySQL appear instead). He has no Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, or meaningful Capital Markets domain depth — RIDE Capital is investment-tech adjacent but focused on German retail investment products, not capital-markets trading systems. The optional desirables (Summit, Kafka/MQ, Python) are also unmet. While his engineering fundamentals and communication track record are solid, the core mandatory requirements are not satisfied.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) across multiple roles
Brief but real C++ exposure at National Instruments and academic wave-field synthesis project shows foundational familiarity with the language
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing history evidenced by conference talks, workshops, and open-source leadership
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months professionally) and there is no evidence of sustained Windows-platform C++ development required by the role
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database work is dominated by MongoDB and MySQL with no enterprise RDBMS depth
Zero Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, or Capital Markets trading-system domain knowledge
